content: Date 2026-02-06. Nightly ETL latency grew from 4h to 7h over the last 3 days. Baseline: pipeline starts 02:00, used to finish ~06:00 (4h). Now finishes ~09:00 (7h). Short suspicion: unindexed joins in the orders mart (the join between orders and shipments looks suspect). We run CTAS into orders_mart partitioned by day; enrichment step joins stg_orders -> stg_shipments on order_id, and also joins customers for name/segment. Everything runs on Postgres 14 on an RDS-like instance with 128GB RAM. I want to walk through causes and decisions.
When exactly did the latency jump start (date/time), and were there any schema or deploy changes around that time?

I first noticed the jump on 2026-02-03 (run for 2026-02-03 night finished at 09:10). No ETL code deploys since 2026-01-28. No schema migrations in prod since 2026-01-20. Data growth: stg_shipments row count went from ~40M in November to ~110M today. The join is orders.id -> shipments.order_id; shipments has only PK on id, no index on order_id. EXPLAIN ANALYZE from 2026-02-05 showed a sequential scan on shipments producing ~110M rows and a hash join with a 58GB hash build that spilled to disk. The join step is adding ~2 hours compared to baseline.
Do you have the EXPLAIN ANALYZE text handy (the actual plan + timing), or at least the node where the spill occurs and work_mem setting?

Yes: EXPLAIN showed Hash Join (cost huge) -> Hash (build) on stg_shipments seq scan 110,234,567 rows, actual 110,234,567 rows, hash size ~58,134,000 kB, spilled to disk. work_mem is default session-level 16MB. DB instance total memory 128GB, max_connections 200, parallel_workers_per_gather is 2. I didn't change planner settings. Rebuilding an index on shipments is scary: table size ~1.2TB, index build estimate 3–5h and ~40GB temp space; CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY seems required.

Which fix do you want prioritized: 1) CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY on shipments(order_id) as a quick DB fix, 2) tweak planner/work_mem/parallel settings for the ETL session, or 3) refactor ETL to incremental/filtered joins (longer work)?

Weighing options: 1) Index CONCURRENTLY: pros — should eliminate seq scan and hash build, estimated runtime 3–5h, cons — heavy IO during build, needs temp space, replication lag risk. 2) Increase work_mem for ETL session (set to 512MB–1GB) and increase parallel_workers_per_gather to 4: pros — immediate less spilling, cons — may still scan 110M rows and not as good as index. 3) Refactor ETL to incremental joins and pre-filter shipments by date/shipment_status: pros — long-term robust, cons — ~2 weeks dev (ticket). Short-term plan: combine 1 + 2 now, long-term plan 3. Proposed immediate actions: schedule CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY on shipments(order_id) during maintenance window 2026-02-07 22:00–04:00; set session work_mem to 512MB for ETL, parallel_workers_per_gather=4 for the ETL window; open ticket ETL-142 with deadline 2026-02-20 to implement incremental joins and pre-filtering.
Okay. One clarifying question: do you have a rollback/stop condition for the index build (replication lag, IOPS) and who will be on-call during the window?

Yes: snapshot DB before starting. Monitor CloudWatch: IOPS, CPU, and replica lag. If replica lag > 60s or IOPS > 70% baseline for >15 minutes, we will pause (pg_cancel_backend for the index process if needed) and abort the run — fallback is to resume the old schedule and revert to increased work_mem only. Camille (DB lead) will be on-call for the window and will start at 21:30 on 2026-02-07. I'll tag ticket ETL-142 and assign Camille, deadline 2026-02-20, and note the mitigation steps.
Sounds coherent. Final quick check: confirm snapshot time and CloudWatch metrics thresholds you'll use in the runbook?

Snapshot at 21:45 on 2026-02-07. CloudWatch thresholds: replica lag > 60s, avg read IOPS > 70% of baseline for 15m, CPU > 85% for 15m — any of those triggers abort. Runbook steps: 1) snapshot 21:45, 2) start CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY 22:00, 3) monitor metrics 22:00–04:00, 4) if abort, run pg_cancel_backend for index process and notify #oncall, 5) revert ETL session settings to defaults and rerun ETL with incremental script disabled until index is stable. Ticket ETL-142 updated with this runbook and assigned to Camille.
Good — tag ETL-142, add the runbook thresholds, and run a quick dry EXPLAIN with the new index (once built). End plan.
